content.problemStatement: Write a program to find the remainder when an integer **A** is divided by an integer **B**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the total number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each line contains two integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, find the remainder when A is divided by B, and display it in a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, 1 divided by 2 leaves a remainder of 1. For the second test case, 100 divided by 200 leaves a remainder of 100. For the third test case, 40 divided by 15 leaves a remainder of 10.
For the first test case, 10 divided by 3 leaves a remainder of 1. For the second test case, 7 divided by 2 leaves a remainder of 1.
For the test case, 25 divided by 5 leaves a remainder of 0.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simply using the modulus operator (%) in the given programming languages. The modulus operator returns the remainder of the division of the number before the operator by the number after the operator.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — The time complexity is linear because we are performing a constant amount of work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — The space complexity is constant because we are using a constant amount of space to store the input and output.

code.Python3:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    a, b = map(int, input().split())
    print(a % b)
